page: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by AMD EPYC 9R45 processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is supported by a 75 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across both compute and memory subsystems. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as Geekbench and Passmark CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth is highly rated, reaching 1039.86 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 430.64 GB/sec for large-block write operations. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in the Passmark memory score, which falls into the average category, contrasting with the top-tier raw memory bandwidth.

This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require massive parallel processing without GPU acceleration. It demonstrates top-tier capabilities in database operations, achieving 439.2 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, and excels in static web serving throughput at 197.64 GB/sec. Additionally, the processor's vector extensions support efficient LLM inference prompt processing, placing in the top 10% for small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds range from average to strong. The bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and eliminates virtualization overhead, making it a cost-efficient option for large-scale enterprise databases, memory-cached applications, and heavy compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, powered by AMD EPYC 9R45 processors running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network performance is anchored by a 75 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark results place the server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and large-block memory bandwidth, though its Passmark memory score remains in the average tier. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale databases, and parallel compilation tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and compute performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server featuring 192 AMD EPYC cores and 1536 GB DDR5 RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform featuring 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 1.5 TB of high-speed DDR5 RAM for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated physical AMD EPYC cores operating at 4.5 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU and database benchmarks
Optimized for large-scale databases, memory caching, and heavy compilation
